zone_6b_notes: Direct sow after last frost when soil is warm — mid-May in Farmington. One of Missouri's best summer annuals; thrives in heat that wilts other flowers. Cutting regularly (every 3–4 days) keeps plants producing abundantly from June until frost. Benary's Giant series produces large dinner-plate blooms; Profusion series gives massive plant coverage with smaller blooms and better mildew resistance. Powdery mildew is common in late summer — plant in full sun with good air circulation. Monarch and swallowtail butterfly magnet. Direct sow only — transplant shock sets them back significantly.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow mid-May after soil warms

water: moderate — 1 inch/week at base; avoid wetting foliage to reduce mildew
